tex:
(a) 
Here are the ingredients needed to make a pasta bake to serve 12 people.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|l|}
\hline
$250\,\text{g}$ butter \\
$600\,\text{g}$ pasta \\
$460\,\text{g}$ mushrooms \\
$280\,\text{g}$ cheese \\
$800\,\text{ml}$ milk \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(i)  Find the mass of the cheese as a percentage of the mass of the mushrooms.
\newline

 Find the mass of butter needed to make a pasta bake to serve 18 people.
\newline

 Monica has 2.2 litres of milk and $1.5\,\text{kg}$ of each other ingredient.
\newline

Calculate the greatest number of people she can serve with pasta bake.
\newline

In 2019, a packet of pasta cost \$2.40.
\newline

This was an increase of 25\% of the cost of a packet in 2018.
\newline

(i)  Work out the cost in 2018.
\newline

 In 2020, the cost of a packet increased by 15\% from the cost in 2019.
\newline

Work out the total percentage increase in the cost of a packet from 2018 to 2020.
\newline

t

Pasta is sold in packets with width $11.5\,\text{cm}$, correct to the nearest $0.5\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

A shop places these packets in a single line on a shelf of length $2\,\text{m}$, correct to the nearest $0.1\,\text{m}$.
\newline

Find the maximum number of these packets that will fit along this shelf.
\newline

You must show all your working.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

This was an increase of 25\% of the cost of a packet in 2018.
\newline

\newline

In 2020, the cost of a packet increased by 15\% from the cost in 2019.
\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

Find the maximum number of these packets that will fit along this shelf.
\newline

qtype: indices simplification, sequences (nth term, terms from nth term), solving linear equations
tex:
(a) 
Simplify fully.
\newline

(i)  $p^3 \times p^{11}$

 $\dfrac{18m^6}{3m^2}$

 $\left( \dfrac{27x^9 y^{27}}{64} \right)^{-\frac{1}{3}}$

A sequence has $n$th term $3n^2$.
\newline

Write down the first 3 terms of this sequence.
\newline

{---} ,  ,][]

Find the $n$th term for each of these sequences.
\newline

(i)  13,   16,   19,   22,   25,   \ldots

 3,   17,   55,   129,   251,   \ldots

Solve.
\newline
\[\frac{3x - 22}{4} = 23\]

Use the quadratic formula to solve $3x^2 + 8x - 20 = 0$.
\newline

Show all your working and give your answers correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

{---} , $x =$][]

content.content:
Simplify fully.
\newline
$p^3 \times p^{11}$

A sequence has $n$th term $3n^2$.
\newline

Write down the first 3 terms of this sequence.
\newline

Find the $n$th term for each of these sequences.
\newline
13, \quad 16, \quad 19, \quad 22, \quad 25, \quad \ldots
3, \quad 17, \quad 55, \quad 129, \quad 251, \quad \ldots

Solve.
\newline
\[\frac{3x - 22}{4} = 23\]

Use the quadratic formula to solve $3x^2 + 8x - 20 = 0$.
\newline

Show all your working and give your answers correct to 2 decimal places.
\newline

tex:
The height, $h\,\text{cm}$, of each of 100 plants is recorded.
\newline

The table shows information about the heights of these plants.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|c|c|c|}
\hline
Height ($h\,\text{cm}$) & $10 < h \leqslant 15$ & $15 < h \leqslant 25$ & $25 < h \leqslant 40$ & $40 < h \leqslant 60$ & $60 < h \leqslant 70$ \\
\hline
Frequency & 8 & 18 & 28 & 33 & 13 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
(a) 
Complete the histogram to show this information.
\newline

The first two blocks have been drawn for you.
\newline

Calculate an estimate of the mean height.
\newline

content.content:
Complete the histogram to show this information.
\newline

The first two blocks have been drawn for you.
\newline

Calculate an estimate of the mean height.
\newline

stem.content:
The height, $h\,\text{cm}$, of each of 100 plants is recorded.
\newline

The table shows information about the heights of these plants.
\newline

tex:
The diagram shows a water trough in the shape of a prism.
\newline

The prism has a cross-section in the shape of an isosceles trapezium.
\newline

The trough is completely filled with water.
\newline

(a) 
Show that the volume of water in the trough is 206.4 litres.
\newline

The water from the trough is emptied at a rate of $600\,\text{ml}$ per second.
\newline

Calculate the time taken, in minutes and seconds, for the trough to be emptied.
\newline

{---} minutes][seconds]

All the water from the trough is emptied into a vertical cylindrical tank.
\newline

The depth of the water in the tank is $84\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

(i)  Calculate the radius of the tank.
\newline

 The tank is 60\% full.
\newline

Calculate the height of the tank.
\newline

t

A steel rod $AM$ is placed inside the empty water trough as shown in the diagram.
\newline

$A$ is a vertex at the base of the isosceles trapezium and $M$ is the midpoint of the top edge on the opposite face.
\newline

Calculate the length of the steel rod, $AM$.
\newline

content.content:
Show that the volume of water in the trough is 206.4 litres.
\newline

The water from the trough is emptied at a rate of $600\,\text{ml}$ per second.
\newline

Calculate the time taken, in minutes and seconds, for the trough to be emptied.
\newline

All the water from the trough is emptied into a vertical cylindrical tank.
\newline

The depth of the water in the tank is $84\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

Calculate the radius of the tank.
\newline

The tank is 60\% full.
\newline

Calculate the height of the tank.
\newline

A steel rod $AM$ is placed inside the empty water trough as shown in the diagram.
\newline

$A$ is a vertex at the base of the isosceles trapezium and $M$ is the midpoint of the top edge on the opposite face.
\newline

Calculate the length of the steel rod, $AM$.
\newline

The diagram shows a water trough in the shape of a prism.
\newline

The prism has a cross-section in the shape of an isosceles trapezium.
\newline

The trough is completely filled with water.
\newline

qtype: formula substitution and rearrangement, solving linear inequalities, graphical inequalities
tex:
(a) 
$P = 5k^2 - 7$

(i)  Find the value of $P$ when $k = 3$.
\newline

 Rearrange the formula to make $k$ the subject.
\newline

t
(i)  Solve.
\newline
\[x - 3 \leqslant 5x + 7\]

 Show your answer to **part (b)(i)** on the number line.
\newline

The line $y = 16$ is drawn on the grid.
\newline

The region $R$ satisfies the following inequalities.
\newline
\[y \geqslant 16 \qquad x > 2 \qquad 2x + 3y \geqslant 72 \qquad y \leqslant 32 - x\]

(i)  By drawing three more lines and shading the region **not required**, find and label region $R$.
\newline

 Find the integer coordinates $(x, y)$ in the region $R$ that give the maximum value of $2x + y$.
\newline

{---} ,  )][]

content.content:
Find the value of $P$ when $k = 3$.
\newline

Rearrange the formula to make $k$ the subject.
\newline

Solve.
\newline
\[x - 3 \leqslant 5x + 7\]

Show your answer to \textbf{part (b)(i)} on the number line.
\newline

The line $y = 16$ is drawn on the grid.
\newline

The region $R$ satisfies the following inequalities.
\newline
\[y \geqslant 16 \qquad x > 2 \qquad 2x + 3y \geqslant 72 \qquad y \leqslant 32 - x\]

By drawing three more lines and shading the region \textbf{not required}, find and label region $R$.
\newline

Find the integer coordinates $(x, y)$ in the region $R$ that give the maximum value of $2x + y$.
\newline

tex:
Regan is playing a game with these six number cards.
\newline

(a) 
She takes two cards at random, without replacement, and **multiplies** the two numbers to give a score.
\newline

Find the probability that

(i)  the score is 35

 the score is a positive number.
\newline

Regan now takes three cards at random from the six cards, without replacement, and **adds** the three numbers to give a total.
\newline

Find the probability that her total is 5.
\newline

content.content:
She takes two cards at random, without replacement, and \textbf{multiplies} the two numbers to give a score.
\newline

Find the probability that
the score is 35

the score is a positive number.
\newline

Regan now takes three cards at random from the six cards, without replacement, and \textbf{adds} the three numbers to give a total.
\newline

Find the probability that her total is 5.
\newline

stem.content:
Regan is playing a game with these six number cards.
\newline

tex:
$A$, $B$ and $C$ are points on the circle, centre $O$.
\newline

\newline

$AC = 10\,\text{cm}$, $AB = 9.5\,\text{cm}$ and $BC = 7.7\,\text{cm}$.
\newline

(a) 
Show that angle $ABC = 70.2°$, correct to 1 decimal place.
\newline

Find

(i)  angle $AOC$

 angle $ACO$

 angle $ACD$.
\newline

Calculate the radius, $OC$, of the circle.
\newline

Calculate the area of triangle $ABC$ as a percentage of the area of the circle.
\newline

qtype: sketching linear, quadratic, exponential graphs; differentiation for gradient and tangent
tex:
(a) 
Sketch the following graphs.
\newline

On each sketch, indicate any intercepts with the axes.
\newline

(i)  $3x - 4y = 12$

 $y = x^2 - 3x - 4$

 $y = 6^x$

t
(i)  Find the derivative, $\dfrac{\mathrm{d}y}{\mathrm{d}x}$, of $y = 5 + 8x - \dfrac{4}{3}x^3$.
\newline

 Find the gradient of $y = 5 + 8x - \dfrac{4}{3}x^3$ at $x = -1$.
\newline

 A tangent is drawn to the graph of $y = 5 + 8x - \dfrac{4}{3}x^3$.
\newline

The gradient of the tangent is $-28$.
\newline

Find the coordinates of the two possible points where this tangent meets the graph.
\newline

{---} ,  )][]
{---} ,  )][]

tex:
(a) 
$\mathbf{a} = \begin{pmatrix} 1 \\ 2 \end{pmatrix}$ \qquad $\mathbf{b} = \begin{pmatrix} -3 \\ 5 \end{pmatrix}$

(i)  On the grid, draw and label vector $2\mathbf{a}$.
\newline

 On the grid, draw and label vector $(\mathbf{a} - \mathbf{b})$.
\newline

t

$OABC$ is a trapezium with $OA$ parallel to $CB$.
\newline

$M$ is the midpoint of $CB$ and $N$ is the point on $AB$ such that $AN : NB = 1 : 2$.
\newline

$O$ is the origin, $\overrightarrow{OA} = \mathbf{p}$, $\overrightarrow{OC} = \mathbf{q}$ and $\overrightarrow{CB} = \dfrac{3}{4}\mathbf{p}$.
\newline

(i)  Find, in terms of $\mathbf{p}$ and/or $\mathbf{q}$, in its simplest form

(b) $\overrightarrow{OB}$

(c) $\overrightarrow{AB}$

(d) $\overrightarrow{MN}$.
\newline

 $OA$ and $MN$ are extended to meet at $G$.
\newline

Find the position vector of $G$ in terms of $\mathbf{p}$.
\newline
